title: Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary
content:
{{Top|rwm|ref|imp|italics=1}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:Ep2VDcover.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Attack of the Clones:
The Visual Dictionary'' |author=[[David West Reynolds (real person)|David West Reynolds]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor=*[[Simon Beecroft]] *[[Jonathan W. Rinzler]] |publisher=[[Dorling Kindersley|DK Publishing, Inc.]] |release date=[[April 23]], [[2002]]{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/ref/news20020227.html|text=Book Preview: DK Episode II Books}} |type=Hardcover |pages=64 |dimensions= |weight= |isbn=9780789485885 |series=''[[Star Wars Visual Dictionaries]]'' |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary''''' is the third book in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars Visual Dictionaries|Visual Dictionary]] series. It describes and exhibits the elements of [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']]. It was republished as part of ''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]''. It gives away no spoilers from ''Attack of the Clones''. ==Publisher's summary== ''Super-sharp annotated photos and authoritative text reveal the world of ''Star Wars: Attack of the Clones'' including the characters, creatures, droids and equipment.'' ==Contents== *The Growing Darkness *[[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] *[[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] *The [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] *[[Droid/Legends|Droids]] *[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] *[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] *[[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell]] *[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Underlevels]] *[[Outlander Club/Legends|Outlander Nightclub]] *[[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter's]] [[Dex's Diner/Legends|Diner]] *[[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] *[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] *[[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] *[[AA-9 Coruscant freighter/Legends|Freighter]] Trampers *[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Naberrie]] *[[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] *[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone Trooper]] Growth *Clone Trooper Equipment *[[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] – [[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty Hunter]] *Jango and [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] *The [[Lars family/Legends|Lars Family]] *[[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] *[[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] *The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] *[[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosian]] Aristocracy *[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] [[Battle droid/Legends|Battle Droids]] *Geonosian Drones *[[Petranaki arena|Arena]] Beasts *[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] in [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle]] *[[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsaber]] Combat *Acknowledgments ==Continuity== *The book states that Anakin studied [[Form IV/Legends|Form IV]] of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]], while in all other sources, Anakin is a practitioner of [[Form V/Legends|Form V]]. *[[Barriss Offee/Legends|Barriss Offee's]] tattoos are erroneously stated to be from [[Chalactan/Legends|Chalactan]] nature. The [[MedStar Duology]], however, correctly stated that her tattoos were from [[Mirialan/Legends|Mirialan]] nature.[[MedStar Duology]] *[[Elan Sel'Sabagno/Legends|Elan Sel'Sabagno]] is shown with [[Ear/Legends|ears]], while in the film he has [[Antennapalps/Legends|antennapalps]]. While Sel'Sabagno originally had ears in the film, these were later removed by [[Industrial Light & Magic]] according to [[Pablo Hidalgo]], thus [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]]ing his appearance in the film. Anyway, the Sel'Sabagno toys were released with ears instead of antennapalps.{{Twitter|pablohidalgo|status/981652528931549186|[[Pablo Hidalgo]]|quote=Today's Star Wars Show reminds me: Elan Sleazebaggano was altered in postproduction, too late for toys in the works to change. The proper Elan has no ears. Reference photography didn't have this digital alteration. Toys had a running change to remove his ears.|archivefile=File:HidalgoSleazebaggano.png}} *As in some early scripts for the film, [[Jamillia/Legends|Jamillia's]] name was spelled "Jamilla."{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=158725&start=75#11220749|thread=Spelling Discrepancies|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-06-06|time=9:57 AM|quote=Jamillia. Some of the early scripts used both spellings, though I agree the pronunciation in the film sounds more like 'Jamilla.' }} *[[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]] is named [[Gilramos Libkath]]. *[[Zam Wesell/Legends|Zam Wesell's]] [[Gender/Legends|gender]], as with most other guidebooks, was listed as "unknown." However, her [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry confirmed that she was female.{{DB|character|zamwesell|Wesell, Zam}} The comic-book one-shot ''[[Star Wars: Zam Wesell]]'' hinted that Wesell was "sometimes" female due to being a [[Clawdite/Legends|Clawdite]].''[[Star Wars: Zam Wesell]]'' ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary}} ===Editions=== *{{ISBN|978078948588}}; [[April 23]], [[2002]]; [[Dorling Kindersley|DK Publishing, Inc.]]; US hardcover ===Cover gallery=== File:Ep2VDcover.jpg|Cover File:Ep2VDcover_Pl.jpg|Polish edition cover — ''Gwiezdne Wojny - Atak Klónow - Słownik obrazkovy'' File:Episode_II_Visual_Dictionary_RU.jpg|Russian edition cover — ''Звездные войны. Эпизод II: Атака клонов.
